use_codes.desc: TREATMENT OF A CANCER MEDIATED BY AN ANAPLASTIC LYMPHOMA KINASE (ALK)
TREATMENT OF PATIENTS WITH METASTATIC NON-SMALL CELL LUNG CANCER (NSCLC) THAT IS ANAPLASTIC LYMPHOMA KINASE (ALK)-POSITIVE
TREATMENT OF PEDIATRIC PATIENTS 1 YEAR OF AGE AND OLDER AND YOUNG ADULTS WITH RELAPSED OR REFRACTORY, SYSTEMIC ANAPLASTIC LARGE CELL LYMPHOMA (ALCL) THAT IS ANAPLASTIC LYMPHOMA KINASE (ALK)-POSITIVE

METHOD OF REVERSING OR INHIBITING THE PROGRESS OF UNRESECTABLE, RECURRENT, OR REFRACTORY INFLAMMATORY MYOFIBROBLASTIC TUMOR (IMT) THAT IS ALK-POSITIVE IN ADULT AND PEDIATRIC PATIENTS 1 YEAR OF AGE AND OLDER

indications.text:
XALKORI is indicated for the treatment of patients with metastatic non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) whose
tumors are anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) or ROS1-positive as detected by an FDA-approved test [see
Clinical Studies (14.1 and 14.2)].

XALKORI is indicated for the treatment of patients with locally advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung
cancer (NSCLC) that is anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK)-positive as detected by an FDA-approved test.
This indication is based on response rate. There are no data available demonstrating improv...
